title: theme — trait composition
description: Two owned traits + composed foundation / tenancy traits on the three aggregates.

owned.purpose: Applied to any downstream row that needs a foreign-key reference to a Theme (currently only ThemeTokenOverride). Registers the theme_id FK + adds a $model->theme() belongsTo relation + adds a query scope $query->forTheme(Theme $t).
Applied to the Theme model. Manages the tokens_snapshot lifecycle — freeze() to resolve the cascade at activation, isFrozen() query, thaw() (for internal-only rollback tooling; not exposed via HTTP).

foundation.trait: HasUlids (with HasPrefixedUlid variant for typed prefixes)

usedBy: ThemePreset (nullable tenant_id — trait applied but nullable behavior configured)

audit.notes: Every mutation captured for the audit lane (7y compliance retention).

activity.notes: Tenant-facing activity feed. ThemeTokenOverride does NOT compose this — override churn is high-volume; noise-to-signal is bad in the activity UI.

composition.note: Trait order matters — BelongsToTenant must come FIRST so downstream traits (BelongsToTheme, HasAudit) observe the tenant context in their booted() hooks. See .kiro/steering/hierarchy.md §14 (trait composition rules).
